title: Clean Fuels Weekly Commentary: LCFS Spot Prices Surge WoW; Type 1 Volumes Also Up
content:
This Weekâ€™s Highlights

(2nd February â€“ 8th February 2026)
  • A total of 576,307 credits were exchanged as Type 1 trades, an increase of 42.94% week-over-week (WoW), while all non-zero trades decreased 49.80% WoW and a total of 774,610 non-zero credits were traded in CA LCFS.
  • The weekly average CA LCFS credit price stood at $64.22/MT for Type 1 transfers, up by 3.05% WoW; prices of all non-zero transfers fell by 1.14%, price stood at $63.46/MT.
  • The average LCFS spot credit price rose by 10.50% WoW; on 10th February, the spot price stood at $71.75/MT, and the benchmark price stood at $73.13/MT.
  • D3 RIN prices remained unchanged WoW, while D4, D5 and D6 RIN prices increased by 4.76%, 4.80% and 3.23% WoW respectively

cCarbonâ€™s Market View

Last week, Californiaâ€™s administration proposed a $200 million EV rebate program in the 2026â€“27 budget, offering point-of-sale rebates for first-time EV buyers, with automakers required to match state funding dollar-for-dollar, doubling available incentives. Final rebate amounts and eligibility details will be determined by the California Air Resources Board in upcoming rulemaking process.

CA LCFS Weekly Price and Volume Trend

[caption id="attachment_612901" align="alignnone" width="627"] Table 1. Weekly average credit prices and weekly volumes, Source: California ARB[/caption] The average Type 1 transfer price increased by 3.05% WoW, rising from $62.32 to $64.22, while all non-zero transfer prices moved down from $64.19 to $63.46, a 1.14% WoW fall. Type 1 trades totaled 576,307 credits, reflecting a 42.94% WoW increase and total non-zero transaction volumes fell by 49.80% WoW, reaching 774,610 credits for the week. [caption id="attachment_612902" align="alignnone" width="1200"] Fig 1. Weekly average credit prices and weekly volumes, Source: California ARB[/caption]

CA LCFS Daily Average Spot Price

[caption id="attachment_612903" align="alignnone" width="1200"] Fig 2. California LCFS Weekly average spot prices and benchmark (*CC.Info OTC: Numbers are an average of broker prices reported to CC.info)[/caption] Last week, the average LCFS spot credit prices rose by 10.50% WoW, reaching $70.48/MT. On 10th February, the spot price stood at $71.75/MT. The benchmark price, representing the future price of contracts expiring at the end of 2026, stood at $73.13/MT on 10th February.

LCFS Weekly Trade Frequency and Volume

[caption id="attachment_612904" align="alignnone" width="1200"] Fig 3. Trade frequency and volume, Source: California ARB[/caption] As per the data reported by CARB last week, a total of 83 trades took place in the market, including 3 high-volume transactions. The highest reported transfer price was $72.25 for Type 1 and Type 2 transactions involving 25,000 and 2,500 credits respectively. The lowest transfer price was $49.50 for a Type 1 transaction covering 133 credits. The largest trade by volume was a Type 1 transaction of 100,000 credits priced at $63.50, while the smallest was a Type 1 transfer of 22 credits priced at $53.00.

CA LCFS Monthly Volumes and Price Trend

[caption id="attachment_612905" align="alignnone" width="1200"] Fig 4. Total monthly volumes and associated prices, Source: California ARB[/caption] The average Type 1 spot price stood at $64.22 in February so far, rising from $58.59 in January. As of 7th February 2026, trade volumes for the month reached 0.77M.

CA LCFS Weekly Volume Price Trend

[caption id="attachment_612906" align="alignnone" width="1200"] Fig 5. Volume Price Trend indicator for the CA LCFS market, Source: California ARB[/caption] The Volume Price Trend Indicator (VPT) helps identify the parity between supply and demand for stock. The idea behind the indicator is to multiply the market volume of LCFS credits by the percentage change in its price. If the credit price declines, the value of the indicator falls. Across 2nd February â€“ 8th February 2026, total VPT fell by 0.59% WoW, with a fall in all non-zero credit prices for the last week.

Renewable Fuel Standard (RFS): RIN Weekly Price Trend

RINs are credits used for compliance and are the â€œcurrencyâ€ of the RFS program. Renewable fuel producers generate RINs, market participants trade RINs, and obligated parties obtain and then ultimately retire RINs for the compliance list. D3 RIN prices remained unchanged WoW, while D4, D5 and D6 RIN prices increased by 4.76%, 4.80% and 3.23% WoW respectively. D3: Cellulosic biofuel D4: Biomass-based diesel D5: Advanced biofuels (produced from any type of renewable biomass except corn starch ethanol) D6: Conventional renewable biofuel ethanol derived from corn starch, or any other qualifying renewable fuel

Upcoming DatesÂ 

Key Clean Fuels Market Developments This Week

  • 2026-27 California Governorâ€™s Budget Trailer Bill Language: Sustainable Aviation Fuel Tax Credit: Trailer Bill 1365 amends the California Revenue and Taxation Code to create a state tax credit for sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) produced and sold in California, effective January 1, 2026. The bill allows a tax credit against state fuel taxes for qualifying SAF producers, providing $1 per gallon of SAF that meets defined carbon intensity reduction criteria, with the credit increasing by $0.02 per gallon for each additional 1% reduction in emissions below 50% of conventional jet fuel emissions. The credit applies only to SAF produced by the taxpayer claiming it and sold for use within California. It can be claimed on tax returns filed on or after November 1, 2027 for the preceding period, effectively incentivising production and in-state sales of lower-carbon aviation fuel.
  • California Unveils Initial Details of $200M Electric Vehicle Rebate Program: Californiaâ€™s governorâ€™s administration released the first details of a proposed $200 million electric vehicle (EV) rebate program as part of the 2026â€“27 state budget process. Under this plan, the state would work with automakers to offer point-of-sale rebates for first-time EV buyers, requiring participating manufacturers to match the state rebate dollar-for-dollar, effectively doubling the funding available. Program specifics â€” such as the rebate amounts for new and used EVs â€” will be finalized by the California Air Resources Board, with eligibility and implementation details to be developed in upcoming meetings and workshops.
  • Government Expected to Scrap EV Mandate in Favour of New Fuel Efficiency System: Source: The federal government is expected to announce its national automotive strategy that includes scrapping Canada's electric vehicle mandate and replacing it with a new system of fuel efficiency standards and credits, CBC News has learned. Prime Minister Mark Carney has been under pressure from leaders in the auto sector, multiple provinces and the Conservatives to scrap the incremental sales targets requiring 60 per cent of all new cars to be electric by 2030 and 100 per cent by 2035.
  • RFA Welcomes Proposed 45Z Tax Credit Regulation: The Renewable Fuels Association welcomed the Treasury Departmentâ€™s release of proposed regulations for the Section 45Z Clean Fuel Production Tax Credit, as authorized by the 2022 Inflation Reduction Act and amended by last yearâ€™s One Big Beautiful Bill Act. â€œTodayâ€™s 45Z proposed rule is a step in the right direction toward providing the clarity and certainty that ethanol producers are seeking,â€ said RFA President and CEO Geoff Cooper. â€œWe thank the Treasury Department and Trump administration for listening to the input provided by ethanol producers and other stakeholders. The proposal appears to resolve some of the previous confusion around what constitutes a â€˜qualified sale,â€™ and begins to integrate the important improvements to 45Z that resulted from the One Big Beautiful Bill Act, such as the removal of indirect land use change emissions from the carbon intensity scoring framework.
  • Canada Launches Electric Vehicle Affordability Program (EVAP): This new program aims to accelerate electric vehicle (EV) adoption by offering Canadian consumers and businesses incentives to buy or lease eligible vehicle models with a final transaction price up to and including $50,000. There will be no limit to the transaction price for Canadian made EVs. Only EVs that are manufactured in Canada or in the countries with which Canada has existing or future free-trade agreements are eligible for the Program.
  • Washington Introduces Incentives for Zero-Emission Trucks and Equipment: The Washington State Department of Transportation is introducing a new program to help businesses and fleet operators transition to cleaner vehicles. Starting in April, potential buyers can request vouchers through the Washington Zero-Emission Incentive Program, or WAZIP. They must work with pre-approved vehicle and equipment dealerships across the state.
  • In Another Climate Retreat, Canada Scraps EV Sales Mandate: Canada said it was scrapping a national electric vehicle mandate, marking another retreat from climate measures under the Liberal government of Prime Minister Mark Carney. In 2023, under then-Prime Minister Justin Trudeau, Ottawa had mandated that 20% of all vehicles sold in 2026 be emissions-free. The push was unpopular with vehicle manufacturers, who said it imposed unsustainable costs, and looked vulnerable when the new U.S. administration cut support for EVs. Canada will introduce stronger emissions standards for 2027-2032 model years which it says will help achieve a goal of 75% EV sales by 2035 and 90% EV sales by 2040.
